evidences.title: MrBeast's YouTube strategy to earn money | ThoughtLeaders Blog
evidences.content: ‍ ‍ In late 2016, Jimmy Donaldson enrolled in college but lasted a mere 2 weeks before he dropped out, stating: “I’d rather be poor than do anything besides YouTube.” And, just a few months later, MrBeast came up with the idea to show himself counting to 100,000 and the video went viral! According to the YouTuber, it took him 44 hours to create this video but he knew that it would go viral (as of June 2023, this video has over 28 million views!) ‍ ‍ MrBeast quickly came to the conclusion that YouTube’s algorithm favors challenge/stunt-style videos. The aspiring YouTuber created more and more videos following similar stunts and quite quickly amassed a large number of views and subscribers. By the end of 2017, MrBeast reached 1 million subscribers - which was no easy feat at the time. [...] ‍ ‍ Wait, but MrBeast obviously doesn’t speak all those languages. Instead, he took the uber-successful content he already created and simply dubbed the voices into the language of choice. As you can see from the data above, MrBeast’s content is successful also internationally and the ability for viewers to watch the content in their desired language has helped MrBeast grow exponentially. At a...

"Last To Remove Hand, Gets Lamborghini Challenge" Uploaded in December 2018, participants compete to win a Lamborghini by being the last to remove their hand from it.
